points.text: Sidewinder Therapeutics announced a $137 million Series B round to advance newer types of antibody-drug conjugates.
The financing will be used to push bispecific ADCs into clinical testing.
Sidewinder is pursuing bispecific ADCs able to recognize and bind to protein combinations highly expressed on cancer cells.
These complexes consist of one protein receptor that helps drive cancer and another that pulls substances into cells.
The company expects to push its lead program into clinical development in 2027.
Sidewinder said it will focus on cancer indications like colorectal tumors and squamous cell carcinomas.
The startup recently partnered with Lonza’s Synaffix subsidiary to gain access to an ADC technology.
The latest financing was co-led by Frazier Life Sciences and the venture arm of Novartis.
OrbiMed participated in the round and is consistently one of the biotech sector’s most active investors.
Eric Murphy, Sidewinder’s CEO, stated the ADC field is at an inflection point driven by technological breakthroughs.
Sanofi announced mid-stage study results for lunsekimig, a bispecific drug targeting TSLP and IL-13.
Lunsekimig succeeded in Phase 2 studies for moderate-to-severe asthma and chronic rhinitis with nasal polyps.
The therapy failed to meet its main objective in a separate trial for atopic dermatitis.
Boehringer Ingelheim announced plans to evaluate a PD-L1/VEGF-A bispecific antibody in small cell lung cancer.
Oxford BioTherapeutics entered a strategic collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb to discover T-cell engager therapies.
Oxford BioTherapeutics includes Bispecific Antibodies in its portfolio of antibody-based therapies.
Sanofi acquired the technology for lunsekimig from biotechnology company Ablynx.
Two Phase 3 trials for lunsekimig are underway in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
The collaboration between Oxford BioTherapeutics and Bristol Myers Squibb focuses on solid tumours.
Analysts forecast $3 billion in peak yearly sales for lunsekimig based on respiratory diseases positioning.
Zymeworks Inc. continues to advance its portfolio of multifunctional biotherapeutics, including the HER2-targeted bispecific antibody Ziihera (zanidatamab-hrii).
Zymeworks appointed Kristin Stafford as Chief Financial Officer effective April 1, 2026, to support strategic priorities and pipeline advancement.
Additional leadership appointments include Adam Schayowitz as Head of R&D and Scott Platshon as Chief Business Officer announced on April 9, 2026.
Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. announced that its PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody Opamtistomig (LBL-024) has advanced to the expansion phase of a Phase II trial.
The Opamtistomig Phase II trial focuses on the first-line treatment of advanced biliary tract cancer (BTC) following positive safety and preliminary efficacy results.
A safety run-in cohort of 20 patients completed preliminary evaluation showing the drug in combination with chemotherapy was generally well tolerated.
Zymeworks has entered into agreements with BeOne Medicines Ltd. and Jazz Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited granting exclusive rights to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
Opamtistomig received Fast Track Designation from the FDA and Orphan Drug Designation from the European Commission for the treatment of extrapulmonary neuroendocrine carcinoma in January 2026.
Zymeworks utilizes its proprietary Azymetric™ technology to engineer Ziihera, a bispecific antibody targeting HER2.
Leads Biolabs developed Opamtistomig using its proprietary X-Body bispecific platform featuring a 2:2 molecular format.
MacroGenics received FDA removal of partial clinical hold on the LINNET study for lorigerlimab, a PD-1 × CTLA-4 bispecific DART molecule.
The LINNET study is a Phase 2 monotherapy trial evaluating lorigerlimab in patients with platinum-resistant ovarian cancer or clear cell gynecologic cancer.
MacroGenics anticipates reporting initial clinical data from the LINNET study in mid-2026 following the resolution of the clinical hold.
MacroGenics and Gilead are advancing MGD024, a clinical-stage CD123 × CD3 bispecific DART molecule, in an ongoing dose escalation study in AML and MDS.
Cue Biopharma received a $7.5 million preclinical milestone payment from Boehringer Ingelheim under their collaboration and license agreement for CUE-501.
MacroGenics reported cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of $189.9 million as of December 31, 2025, with cash runway guidance into late 2027.
MacroGenics plans to submit an Investigational New Drug (IND) application for MGC030, a first-in-class topoisomerase I inhibitor-based ADC, in the third quarter of 2026.
Lucinda Warren has been appointed interim president and chief executive officer of Cue Biopharma following the departure of Usman Azam.
MacroGenics remains eligible to receive up to $1.6 billion in future milestones related to its collaboration with Gilead involving three programs.
Cue Biopharma's lead autoimmune asset CUE-401 is designed as a tolerogenic bifunctional molecule combining a TGF-beta breathing-mask moiety with an interleukin 2 mutein.
NovaBridge Biosciences secured FDA alignment on givastomig’s potential eligibility for an accelerated approval pathway in first-line gastroesophageal cancer patients.
Givastomig is a bispecific Claudin 18.2 X 4-1BB antibody developed in partnership with ABL Bio.
Phase 1b data for givastomig demonstrated a 75% objective response rate and a 16.9-month median progression-free survival in evaluable patients.
Sanofi reported that lunsekimig, a novel bispecific nanobody, met primary and key secondary endpoints in phase 2 respiratory studies for asthma and CRSwNP.
Samsung Biologics highlights structural innovation and enhanced manufacturability as critical factors in redefining bispecific antibody development.
Bispecific antibodies enable enhanced therapeutic efficacy through dual-targeting but present significant manufacturing and purification challenges.
NovaBridge intends to initiate a registrational Phase 3 combination trial for givastomig as early as Q4 2026 using objective response rate as a primary endpoint.
Sanofi’s lunsekimig program targets two chronic respiratory conditions with positive phase 2 clinical outcomes.
Samsung Biologics offers CDMO services including cell line development to final aseptic fill/finish for biologics including complex modalities.
The industry faces growing biomanufacturing demands requiring optimized service to fit client needs for complex modalities like bispecifics.
Sidewinder Therapeutics announced a $137 million Series B financing round on April 8, 2026.
The funding will be used to advance newer types of antibody-drug conjugates, or ADCs, into clinical testing.
Sidewinder is pursuing bispecific ADCs able to recognize and bind to protein combinations that are highly expressed on cancer cells.
These complexes consist of one protein receptor that helps drive cancer, and another that pulls substances into cells.
The company believes focusing on these combinations could lead to a more targeted and effective attack on tumors.
Sidewinder said it will focus on cancer indications like colorectal tumors and squamous cell carcinomas that affect large populations but have limited treatment options.
Sidewinder was founded in 2023 with Series A backing from OrbiMed.
Including the latest funding, the startup has now raised a total of $162 million.
Industry storylines within the reporting period also referenced a prior rejection of a Regeneron cancer bispecific.
Sanofi announced mid-stage study results for lunsekimig, a bispecific drug targeting TSLP and IL-13, on April 7, 2026.
Lunsekimig met its main and key secondary goals in Phase 2 studies evaluating the treatment in moderate-to-severe asthma and chronic rhinitis with nasal polyps.
The therapy failed to meet its main objective in a separate trial in atopic dermatitis, failing to meet a certain threshold of skin clearance compared to a placebo.
Sanofi is under growing pressure to find a successor to Dupixent, a top-selling drug co-developed with Regeneron.
Boehringer Ingelheim announced an evaluation of an innovative combination of DLL3-targeting T-cell engager plus PD-L1/VEGF-A bispecific antibody in small cell lung cancer.
Oxford BioTherapeutics entered a strategic collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb on April 9, 2026, to discover and develop next-generation T-cell engager therapies for solid tumours.
The Oxford BioTherapeutics collaboration leverages the company's proprietary OGAP®-Verify discovery platform to identify novel, tumour-selective targets.
Oxford BioTherapeutics will receive an upfront payment and may be eligible to receive milestone payments, as well as royalties on net sales from Bristol Myers Squibb.
Sanofi noted that treatment benefits observed in asthma with lunsekimig were seen regardless of biomarker status.
Two Phase 3 trials for lunsekimig are underway in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, another lung condition.
Zymeworks Inc. manages a portfolio of licensed healthcare assets while developing a diverse pipeline of novel, multifunctional biotherapeutics.
Zymeworks engineered and developed Ziihera, a HER2-targeted bispecific antibody using the Zymeworks’ proprietary Azymetric™ technology.

Zymeworks has entered into separate agreements with BeOne Medicines Ltd. and Jazz Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited granting each exclusive rights to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
Nanjing Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. announced that its proprietary PD-L1/4-1BB bispecific antibody, Opamtistomig, has successfully completed the safety run-in stage of a Phase II clinical trial.
The Phase II clinical trial for Opamtistomig focuses on the first-line treatment of advanced biliary tract cancer (BTC).
A total of 20 patients were included in the safety run-in cohort for the Opamtistomig study and completed preliminary evaluation.
Results demonstrated that Opamtistomig in combination with chemotherapy was generally well tolerated with a favorable safety profile and no new safety signals observed.
Opamtistomig is a potential first-in-class bispecific antibody that simultaneously targets PD-L1 and the co-stimulatory receptor 4-1BB.
Opamtistomig received Fast Track Designation by the FDA and Orphan Drug Designation by the European Commission for the treatment of extrapulmonary neuroendocrine carcinoma in January 2026.
Leads Biolabs utilizes its proprietary X-Body bispecific platform to develop drug candidates with differentiated clinical outcomes.
NovaBridge Biosciences secured FDA alignment on givastomig’s potential eligibility for an accelerated approval pathway in first-line gastroesophageal cancer.
The FDA confirmed givastomig’s potential eligibility for an accelerated approval pathway building on positive data from the Phase 1b combination trial.
NovaBridge expects to initiate a registrational Phase 3 combination trial as early as Q4 2026 using objective response rate as a primary endpoint.
Sanofi’s lunsekimig, a novel bispecific nanobody, met primary and key secondary endpoints in phase 2 respiratory studies in asthma and CRSwNP.
Bispecific antibodies enable enhanced therapeutic efficacy through dual-targeting but pose significant manufacturing and purification challenges requiring innovative solutions.
Givastomig is a bispecific Claudin 18.2 X 4-1BB antibody with the potential to be a first-in-class therapeutic in Her-2 negative, CLDN 18.2 positive, PD-L1-positive gastroesophageal cancer.
Phase 1b dose expansion data showed givastomig produced a 75% objective response rate across a wide range of PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 expression levels.
Samsung Biologics offers solutions for redefining bispecific antibody development with structural innovation and enhanced manufacturability.
Merck has initiated a pivotal phase 2b/3 trial for Tiespectus, an investigational bispecific tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor, to treat neovascular age-related macular degeneration.
The EMERALD-3 phase III trial demonstrated that Imfinzi plus Imjudo combined with lenvatinib and TACE improved progression-free survival in embolisation-eligible unresectable liver cancer.
Five years after an initial lupus breakthrough, CAR-T therapy continues to surprise autoimmunity researchers with sustained remission in treated patients.
There is a significant level of interest from pharma in the area of autoimmune disease treatment using advanced cell therapies.
Georg Schett and Fabian Müller at the University of Erlangen-Nuremberg successfully treated a young patient with lupus using chimeric antigen receptor T cells.
The EMERALD-3 trial results were described as statistically significant and clinically meaningful for the liver cancer indication.
MacroGenics received regulatory clearance to resume enrollment in the phase 2 LINNET study for lorigerlimab following a partial clinical hold.
Lorigerlimab is an investigational bispecific DART molecule designed to simultaneously target PD-1 and CTLA-4 for gynecologic cancers.
Research from Cedars-Sinai Cancer Center investigates T cell immune fitness to predict outcomes for patients treated with bispecific T cell engager antibodies.
Mechanistic studies suggest moving bispecific antibodies to earlier treatment settings, such as consolidation therapy, to improve clinical outcomes in hematologic malignancies.
The FDA’s Office of Oncologic Diseases reviewed safety data including grade 4 neutropenia and myocarditis events to mitigate risks in the LINNET trial.
Longitudinal immune monitoring is being utilized to define T cell immune fitness as a biomarker for predicting treatment responses to bispecific immunotherapies.
Clinical data updates for the LINNET study are expected in the second half of 2026 to provide insight into efficacy in platinum-resistant and clear cell gynecologic malignancies.
Real-world evidence in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma indicates that bispecific antibodies are emerging as part of the evolving therapeutic landscape alongside monoclonal antibodies.
Safety concerns previously paused the LINNET trial due to serious safety events including a grade 5 fatal event linked to neutropenia and septic shock.
Bispecific antibody development faces challenges regarding sustained responses in patients with acute myeloid leukemia compared to other disease stages.

Imfinzi plus Imjudo — Unresectable Liver Cancer — EMERALD-3 phase III trial demonstrated statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival with lenvatinib and TACE.
University of Erlangen-Nuremberg — CAR-T Therapy — Systemic Lupus Erythematosus — Five-year follow-up confirms sustained remission in initial patient case.

Cedars-Sinai Cancer Center — Bispecific T cell engager antibodies — Acute myeloid leukemia — Research identifies distinct T cell functional states affecting sustained responses.
Sidewinder Therapeutics — Advance precision bispecific ADCs — Lead program targeting protein combinations on cancer cells.

Sanofi — Developing novel bispecific nanobodies for respiratory conditions — Lunsekimig (Bispecific nanobody for asthma and CRSwNP).

Sidewinder Therapeutics — Strategic objective to lead innovation in next-generation bispecific ADCs — Associated program is lead bispecific ADC targeting protein combinations on cancer cells.
Sanofi — Find a successor to Dupixent — Lunsekimig bispecific drug.
Boehringer Ingelheim — Evaluate innovative combination therapy in oncology — DLL3-targeting T-cell engager plus PD-L1/VEGF-A bispecific antibody.
Oxford BioTherapeutics — Advance oncology innovation via proprietary platform — OGAP®-Verify and T-cell engager therapies.
Bristol Myers Squibb — Lead subsequent research, development and commercialisation — T-cell engager therapies for solid tumours.
Zymeworks Inc. — Strategic objective to optimize positive future cash flows from licensed products and support development of innovative medicines — Associated drug Ziihera (zanidatamab-hrii).
Nanjing Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. — Strategic objective to address underserved medical needs in oncology with next-generation immuno-oncology treatments — Associated drug Opamtistomig (LBL-024).
Sanofi Winthrop Industrie — Developing novel bispecific nanobodies for chronic respiratory conditions — Lunsekimig (Bispecific nanobody).

Samsung Biologics — Offering seamless development and manufacturing services from cell line development to final aseptic fill/finish for biologics — S-DUAL™ Brochure and bispecific antibody manufacturing solutions.
Merck — Developing bispecific tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor for ophthalmology indications — Tiespectus.
Imfinzi plus Imjudo Program — Evaluating combination immunotherapy in liver cancer — EMERALD-3 phase III trial.
University of Erlangen-Nuremberg — Advancing CAR-T therapy for autoimmune conditions — Lupus CAR-T program.
MacroGenics — Developing bispecific DART molecules for solid tumors — Lorigerlimab (PD-1/CTLA-4 bispecific).
Cedars-Sinai Cancer Center — Optimizing immunotherapeutic strategies for hematologic malignancies — Bispecific T cell engager antibody research.
Mechanism involves bispecific ADCs recognizing protein combinations on cancer cells.
Targeting includes one protein receptor driving cancer and another that pulls substances into cells.
Lead program is expected to enter clinical development in 2027.
Indications include colorectal tumors and squamous cell carcinomas affecting large populations.
Lunsekimig — Bispecific targeting TSLP and IL-13 — Phase 2 — Moderate-to-severe asthma — Statistically significant reduction in symptom flare-ups.
Lunsekimig — Bispecific targeting TSLP and IL-13 — Phase 2 — Chronic rhinitis with nasal polyps — Reduced size and severity of nasal polyps.
Lunsekimig — Bispecific targeting TSLP and IL-13 — Phase 2 — Atopic dermatitis — Failed to meet skin clearance threshold compared to placebo.
PD-L1/VEGF-A bispecific antibody — Bispecific antibody — Clinical evaluation — Small cell lung cancer — Combination with DLL3-targeting T-cell engager.
Oxford BioTherapeutics programs — Bispecific Antibodies — Preclinical/Discovery — Solid tumours — Leveraging OGAP®-Verify platform.
Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — Mechanism: Simultaneously blocks PD-1/L1-mediated immune suppression and activates 4-1BB co-stimulatory pathway using a 2:2 molecular format.

Opamtistomig (LBL-024) — Trial Phase: Phase II — Patient Population: Advanced biliary tract cancer (first-line) — Results: Safety run-in of 20 patients showed favorable safety profile with no new safety signals and encouraging tumor reduction trends.
Ziihera (zanidatamab-hrii) — Mechanism: HER2-targeted bispecific antibody engineered using Zymeworks’ proprietary Azymetric™ technology.

Lorigerlimab — PD-1 × CTLA-4 bispecific DART molecule — Phase 2 monotherapy — Patients with platinum-resistant ovarian cancer or clear cell gynecologic cancer — LINNET study update planned for mid-2026.
MGD024 — CD123 × CD3 bispecific DART molecule — Clinical-stage dose escalation — Patients with AML and MDS — Advancing in collaboration with Gilead.

CUE-401 — Tolerogenic bifunctional molecule combining TGF-beta breathing-mask moiety with IL-2 mutein — Preclinical/Early development — Designed to regulate proinflammatory mechanisms and induce regulatory T cell differentiation.
Givastomig conditionally activates T cells through the 4-1BB signaling pathway in the tumor microenvironment where Claudin 18.2 is expressed.
Givastomig Phase 1b dose expansion study evaluated 8 mg/kg and 12 mg/kg every two weeks in first-line gastric cancer patients.
Givastomig Phase 1b results showed 75% objective response rate (ORR) with 77% ORR at 8 mg/kg and 73% ORR at 12 mg/kg (n=52 evaluable).
Givastomig Phase 1b results showed durable responses with 16.9-month median progression-free survival (mPFS) and an 82% 6-month landmark PFS rate (n=53 evaluable).
Lunsekimig is a novel bispecific nanobody evaluated in phase 2 respiratory studies for asthma and CRSwNP.
Lunsekimig phase 2 studies met primary and key secondary endpoints in two chronic respiratory conditions.
Drug mechanism involves bispecific ADCs recognizing protein combinations where one receptor drives cancer and another pulls substances into cells.
Development status expects lead program to push into clinical development in 2027.
Patient population targets include individuals with colorectal tumors and squamous cell carcinomas.
Lunsekimig is a bispecific drug that targets two known drivers of inflammation, TSLP and IL-13.
In asthma Phase 2 studies, lunsekimig treatment led to a statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in symptom flare-ups and helped improve lung function.
In chronic rhinitis with nasal polyps Phase 2 studies, lunsekimig reduced the size and severity of nasal polyps as well as related congestion.
The drug was generally well tolerated across the trials, with serious adverse events and discontinuation rates comparable between treatment and placebo recipients.
Boehringer Ingelheim is evaluating a combination of DLL3-targeting T-cell engager plus PD-L1/VEGF-A bispecific antibody in small cell lung cancer.
Oxford BioTherapeutics OGAP®-Verify discovery platform enables highly sensitive identification of oncology targets with improved attributes for drug development.
Opamtistomig utilizes a 2:2 molecular format incorporating two binding domains each for PD-L1 and 4-1BB with an optimized affinity ratio to reverse immune suppression and enhance T-cell activation.
The Phase II trial for Opamtistomig in biliary tract cancer included a safety run-in cohort of 20 patients receiving the bispecific antibody in combination with chemotherapy.
Preliminary efficacy assessments for Opamtistomig indicated encouraging tumor reduction trends in the advanced biliary tract cancer study.
Ziihera is engineered using Zymeworks’ proprietary Azymetric™ technology as a HER2-targeted bispecific antibody.

Opamtistomig has demonstrated first- or best-in-class potential in Phase II or registrational clinical trials across non-small cell lung cancer, small cell lung cancer, and extrapulmonary neuroendocrine carcinoma.
Givastomig mechanism involves conditional activation of T cells through the 4-1BB signaling pathway in the tumor microenvironment where CLDN18.2 is expressed.
Phase 1b dose expansion study utilized givastomig dosed at 8 mg/kg and 12 mg/kg every two weeks in combination with immunochemotherapy.
Evaluable patient population for givastomig Phase 1b data included 52 patients for objective response rate and 53 for progression free survival analysis.
Lunsekimig is characterized as a novel bispecific nanobody targeting respiratory conditions including asthma and CRSwNP.
Bispecific antibodies enable enhanced therapeutic efficacy through dual-targeting but complex structure poses significant manufacturing and purification challenges.
Tiespectus — Bispecific tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor — Phase 2b/3 — Neovascular age-related macular degeneration — Pivotal trial initiated.
Imfinzi plus Imjudo — Dual checkpoint inhibition (PD-L1 and CTLA-4) — Phase III (EMERALD-3) — Embolisation-eligible unresectable liver cancer — Progression-free survival improvement.
CAR-T Therapy — Chimeric antigen receptor T cells — Clinical observation — Systemic Lupus Erythematosus — Five-year remission status.

Lorigerlimab — Bispecific DART molecule targeting PD-1 and CTLA-4 — Phase 2 LINNET study — Platinum-resistant ovarian cancer and clear cell gynecologic cancer — Primary endpoint: Objective response rate (ORR).
Bispecific T cell engager antibodies — Mechanism involves T cell engagement — Acute myeloid leukemia — Research indicates T cell functional states vary by disease stage (diagnosis vs. remission).
Evolving Therapeutic Landscape — Bispecific Antibodies — Relapsed/refractory DLBCL — Real-world evidence identifies bispecific antibodies as emerging therapies alongside CD19 agents.
Sidewinder Therapeutics secured $137 million Series B financing co-led by Frazier Life Sciences and Novartis Venture Arm.
Sidewinder partnered with Lonza’s Synaffix subsidiary to gain access to ADC technology licensed to several firms.
OrbiMed, Astellas Venture Management and multiple other firms involved in the financing round.
Oxford BioTherapeutics — Bristol Myers Squibb — Multi-year selective target collaboration for T-cell engagers.
Sanofi — Ablynx — Technology acquisition for lunsekimig.
Oxford BioTherapeutics — GSK and Roche — Strategic partnerships in 2025.

Zymeworks entered into separate agreements with BeOne Medicines Ltd. (formerly BeiGene, Ltd.) and Jazz Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited granting exclusive rights to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.
MacroGenics and Gilead — Collaboration advancing three programs including MGD024 — MacroGenics eligible for up to $1.6 billion in future milestones and royalties.
Cue Biopharma and Boehringer Ingelheim — Collaboration and License Agreement for CUE-501 — Cue Biopharma to receive $7.5 million preclinical milestone payment.
MacroGenics and Incyte — Commercialization of ZYNYZ (retifanlimab-dlwr) — MacroGenics eligible for up to $540 million in additional milestones.

Givastomig is being jointly developed through a global partnership with ABL Bio, in which NovaBridge is the lead party and shares worldwide rights, excluding Greater China and South Korea, equally with ABL Bio.
NovaBridge owns worldwide rights outside of China to uliledlimab, an anti-CD73 antibody that targets adenosine-driven immunosuppression in cancer.
NovaBridge is the majority shareholder of Visara, Inc., and Visara controls global rights to VIS-101, outside of Greater China and certain countries in Asia.
Samsung Biologics is a fully integrated, end-to-end CDMO partner offering seamless development and manufacturing services from cell line development to final aseptic fill/finish for biologics.
Sidewinder Therapeutics secured $137 million in Series B financing co-led by Frazier Life Sciences and the venture arm of Novartis.
The startup recently partnered with Lonza’s Synaffix subsidiary to gain access to an ADC technology.
Oxford BioTherapeutics and Bristol Myers Squibb entered a multi-year, selective target collaboration with upfront payment, milestone payments, and royalties on net sales.
Sanofi acquired the technology for lunsekimig from biotechnology company Ablynx.
Sanofi co-developed Dupixent with Regeneron, which accounted for about a third of Sanofi’s sales last year.
Oxford BioTherapeutics collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb marks the company’s third partnership with a major pharmaceutical company in the past 12 months.

Zymeworks entered into separate agreements with BeOne Medicines Ltd. and Jazz Pharmaceuticals Ireland Limited granting each exclusive rights to develop and commercialize zanidatamab in different territories.

Givastomig is being jointly developed through a global partnership with ABL Bio, in which NovaBridge is the lead party and shares worldwide rights excluding Greater China and South Korea equally with ABL Bio.
Samsung Biologics operates as a fully integrated, end-to-end CDMO partner offering seamless development and manufacturing services for biologics including bispecific antibodies.
No relevant business developments reported.
